facade_colour: warm red-orange brick with white/cream painted wood trim; decorative wood scrollwork on dormers

hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Second Empire and other Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructed as part of a row with the adjacent property at 61 Bellevue Avenue, the properties retain many of their original building features, including their shared mansard roofline, round-arched dormers with wood scrollwork trim, brick cladding, and single-storey bay windows, and segmentally arched window and door openings at the first storey. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. Each property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: warm red-orange brick with white/cream painted wood trim; decorative wood scrollwork on dormers
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Brick cladding in fair condition; white-painted wood trim around dormers and bay window; mansard slate roof visible with round-arched dormers
photo_observations.window_details: Bay window at ground floor with three sash windows; round-arched dormers at mansard level with decorative wood scrollwork surround; number 59 visible
photo_observations.door_details: Single entry door beside bay window; segmentally arched opening with transom; recessed entry
photo_observations.cornice_details: Decorative cornice at mansard transition; painted wood
photo_observations.bay_window_details: Single-storey three-sided projecting bay at ground floor with three sash windows; white painted wood frame
photo_observations.porch_details: Small stoop with steps at entry door
photo_observations.decorative_details: Round-arched dormer with ornate wood scrollwork trim; segmentally arched openings at first floor; decorative brick corbelling
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Wooden fence at front added; some modern elements visible but original Victorian character largely intact
photo_observations.notes: Matches HCD description well - retains mansard roofline, round-arched dormers with wood scrollwork, bay window, segmentally arched openings

facade_description: 59 Bellevue Ave is a house-form, semi-detached building. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double hung, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, original windows, bay window, cornice.
heritage_summary: Constructed as part of a row with the adjacent property at 61 Bellevue Avenue, the properties retain many of their original building features, including their shared mansard roofline, round-arched dormers with wood scrollwork trim, brick cladding, and single-storey bay windows, and segmentally arched window and door openings at the first storey. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.
